examples.text: مَنْ قَدَّمَهُ طٰهٰ وَمَنْ *** أَهْلُ الْإِسْلَامِ لَهُ أَمَّرَ؟
examples.english: Who did Tāhā place at the forefront and who *** did he assign as a leader for the people of Islam?

examples.text: طٰهٰ يَا نَبِينَا / يَا رَحْمَة الله فِينا
examples.english: Tāhā, O our prophet / O mercy of God upon us

etymology.text: From طَاء (ṭāʔ) + هَاء (hāʔ), the names of the letters ط and ه, in reference to the surah Ta-Ha, named after its “mysterious letters”, sometimes interpreted as referring obliquely to Muhammad.

body.text: [at the commencement of the 20th chapter of the Kur-án] means
&c.]: (K, TA:) and thus it is expl. as occurring in a trad. respecting Moses' hearing the speech of the Lord of Might: (TA:) or, as some say, it is for
tread upon the ground with the soles of both thy feet;
because the Prophet raised one of his feet in prayer: (TA in art.
in the Abyssinian language; (Lth, K, TA;) or so in Syriac, accord. to Katádeh; or so in Nabathæan, accord. to other authorities: (TA:) read
, with the two fet-hahs pronounced fully, it is two letters of the alphabet (
[strangely misunderstood by Freytag as meaning “ quibus maledicitur ”]): (Lth, K, TA:) Ibn-Mes'ood is related to have read
, with the two kesrehs pronounced fully: and Fr says that some divided it, reading
